task_completion.description: Create a new item in Juris-M with the type 'Interview'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Juris-M application is open, a new item is selected in the center pane, and the right-hand info pane shows the 'Item Type' field set to 'Interview'.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct title for the interview
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Title' field in the Juris-M info pane contains exactly 'Oral history interview with John Lewis' (or matching capitalization).

task_completion.description: Enter the Interviewee and Interviewer details
task_completion.visual_evidence: The creator fields in the Juris-M info pane show 'Lewis, John' with the role 'Interviewee' and 'Bond, Julian' with the role 'Interviewer'.

task_completion.description: Enter the Date and Medium details
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Date' field contains 'November 20, 2013' and the 'Medium' field contains 'Transcript' in the Juris-M info pane.

task_completion.description: Enter the URL of the interview
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'URL' field in the Juris-M info pane contains 'https://www.loc.gov/item/2015026020/'.

task_completion.description: Enter the Short Title for the interview
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Short Title' field in the Juris-M info pane contains 'Lewis Interview'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Juris-M graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Juris-M application window with its standard layout (left pane for libraries, center for item lists, right for item details), not a terminal window or a generic text editor.

integrity.description: The agent entered the data into the correct distinct fields rather than pasting everything into a single field.
integrity.visual_evidence: The metadata values are populated in their respective individual fields (Title, Author/Interviewee, Date, etc.) in the right-hand pane, rather than pasted as a single block of text into the 'Extra' or 'Notes' section.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply open a web browser to display the text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final state should show the completed entry inside the Juris-M application, not just a web browser attempting to navigate to the provided (and non-existent) Library of Congress URL.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: The title 'Oral History Interview with John Lewis' and medium 'Transcript' are consistent with real-world oral histories (e.g., from the Southern Oral History Program or UVA Explorations in Black Leadership). However, the specific date (November 20, 2013) and URL (loc.gov/item/2015026020/) provided in the prompt are fictional and will return a 404 error if visited. The agent must enter the exact details provided in the prompt into Juris-M, regardless of their real-world validity. Do not penalize the agent if the URL does not load in a browser; the goal is solely data entry into Juris-M.
